stated_strategic_priorities: Scale private markets advisory and solutions platform by leveraging data analytics across $709B total capital responsibility to differentiate sourcing and underwriting
Expand private wealth distribution channel through StepStone Private Wealth to access high-net-worth and retail-adjacent capital pools with semi-liquid fund structures
Broaden asset class coverage and deepen infrastructure and private credit capabilities through targeted acquisitions and organic fund launches

contextual_market_factors.tam_notes: StepStone operates across the ~$13T global private markets universe, with particular strength in the advisory/solutions segment (estimated ~$3T in outsourced private market allocations by institutional investors). The private wealth channel for alternatives is projected to grow from ~$4T to ~$12T by 2030, representing StepStone's primary growth vector.
contextual_market_factors.governance_notes: Dual-class share structure with Class B shares held by pre-IPO partners carrying 10 votes per share. IPO in September 2020. Public float represents Class A shares with one vote each. Governance structure preserves founder/partner control over long-term strategic direction but limits outside shareholder influence.
contextual_market_factors.regulatory_notes: SEC-registered investment adviser. Subject to Investment Advisers Act. Private wealth products (e.g., SPRING Fund) subject to additional Regulation D and interval fund requirements under the Investment Company Act of 1940. Global advisory activities regulated across multiple jurisdictions including UK FCA, Australian ASIC, and Singapore MAS.
